$comment: Docs: docs/schemas/README.md. Changelog: docs/schemas/CHANGELOG.md. Product: docs/product/leprechaun-nfts.md. Top-level doc map: docs/README.md.
title: Leprechaun NFT onchain gameplay metadata (v1 outline)
description: Draft outline for verifiable onchain or URI-backed JSON. Authority for gameplay fields remains onchain per product spec. Bump schemaVersion when the shape changes; record file edits in docs/schemas/CHANGELOG.md. Semver rules: docs/product/leprechaun-nfts.md.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of this metadata shape (not the token contract). Indexers key migrations on this string.

role.description: Gameplay role; extend enum in minor versions.

rarityTier.description: Ordinal rarity band; higher = scarcer within rules.

setId.description: Stable id for the set across the collection.

setSize.description: Total distinct pieces in the set definition.

synergyThresholdCount.description: Minimum distinct held pieces to unlock set synergy (e.g. 3 of 5).

requiredSiblingTokenIds.description: Optional explicit sibling keys when rules are not purely count-based.

factionId.description: Team/faction key for treasury-war style mechanics.

seasonalSeriesId.description: When series is seasonal_faction, binds token to a specific limited run.

seasonStartUnix.description: Inclusive start of seasonal eligibility window (unix seconds).

seasonEndUnix.description: Exclusive or inclusive end per published rules; document interpretation at bump.

category.description: Bonus channel; v1 examples align with product doc.

valueRaw.description: Signed or unsigned magnitude in fixed-point units; sign policy per category.

scale.description: Denominator for fixed-point interpretation (e.g. 10000 = four decimal places).

valueCapRaw.description: Optional ceiling for stacking or per-tx application.

tags.description: Composability keys for cross-NFT effects; namespace with prefixes in practice.

soulbound.description: Hint: non-transferable or listing-restricted per policy.

metadataMutableByOwner.description: Hint for agents: cosmetic vs gameplay-locked fields.
